title: The Cold Water
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A latchkey kid in 1990s Florida survives a hurricane alone and emerges into a world reframed by loss and unexpected kindness.
long_description:
In the summer of 1992, twelve-year-old River Corso is left alone in a central Florida trailer park when his mother drives to Tampa for a waitressing shift she never comes back from. When Hurricane Celia barrels across the peninsula, River must board up their double-wide by himself, ride out the worst of it in a bathtub, and emerge into a landscape where everything he knows is underwater or gone.

In the aftermath of the storm, River meets Mrs. Delgado, an elderly Cuban-American widow who lost her own home. Together they navigate the wreckage, the shelters, and the long wait for news. As River searches for his missing mother and learns the truth about the night she left, he discovers that survival is not about being tough. It is about letting people in.

The Cold Water is a spare, lyrical debut about the storms we survive and the quiet kindnesses that carry us through. Set against the humid backdrop of 1990s Florida, it is a story of a boy who learns that the coldest water can still be entered, and that coming home means finding people who will hold the door open.
main_characters: River Corso — A twelve-year-old latchkey kid, sharp-eyed and quiet, accustomed to fending for himself. He watches the Weather Channel obsessively and reads library books about sharks.
Mrs. Delgado (Delia Delgado) — A seventy-three-year-old Cuban-American widow who fled Cuba in 1961 with nothing but a sewing machine. She can read a sky better than any meteorologist.
Dina Corso — River's mother. A waitress, smoker, and dreamer who loves her son but does not know how to stay. Her absence is the wound the book circles.
Luis Delgado — Mrs. Delgado's son, a contractor in Miami with gentle hands and a big heart.
Randy Phelps — The trailer park manager, loud and kind, who keeps an inventory of survivors after the storm.
